hook_type: Direct second-person recursion: 'You backed X, now you can back the platform'
primary_text:
You backed a Birchal company. Now you can back Birchal.

324 companies. $234M+. 137,000 of you. Naked Life. Our Cow. Medigrowth. Earthletica. Every raise was funded by retail investors who decided to own a piece of something they believed in.

Birchal is opening its own raise on the platform you built.

Save your spot. You will see the offer the moment it goes live, with time to read it before you decide.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: The platform you built is opening up
description: Save your spot. You will see the offer the moment it goes live.
_drc_playbook: 11 Lifecycle Email Promo / Launch (B2C considered, most-aware, active list)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, most-aware. Existing Birchal investors who have made at least one investment on the platform.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'see the offer', 'read it'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'you will see the offer the moment it goes live' -- informs about timing, does not promise to send the document.
BRAND-EXCLUDED: only verified-good raises (Naked Life, Our Cow, Medigrowth, Earthletica).
Anti-preferential terms (s 738G): 'opening its own raise on the platform you built' -- 'you built' refers to the platform's existence, not differential access. No first-access language.
Insider voice ('of you', 'you built') addresses existing investor list -- compliant since it does not promise different terms vs new waitlist signups.
_drc_assumptions_flagged: Audience is targeted via Birchal CRM list or lookalike. If served to non-investors, 'You backed a Birchal company' creates dissonance -- segment in Meta Ads Manager via custom audience.
'$234M+' is rendered with a literal plus sign for fintech-standard cumulative-and-growing convention; readers may parse as '$234 million plus'.
